description_long: This object is a retro-style CRT computer monitor with a boxy, angular form and a slightly curved glass display. The casing is constructed from a light gray, pixelated-textured plastic, giving it a utilitarian and robust appearance. The front features a thick, polygonal bezel framing the screen, with a single red button located on the lower left of the front panel. The rear of the monitor is bulkier, with a protruding section that likely houses internal electronics and ventilation. The top surface is flat with a small, raised rectangular detail, possibly for ventilation or carrying. The overall design is reminiscent of monitors from the late 20th century, suitable for use in urban or industrial environments.
description: A boxy, gray CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, curved screen, and a single red button on the front. The casing is angular and pixelated, evoking a retro, utilitarian style.
description_view_0: Side view showing the monitor's deep, angular casing and the protruding rear section.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the curved glass screen, thick bezel, and a red button on the lower left.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the angular casing and rear protrusion seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Top view displaying the flat upper surface with a small raised rectangular detail at the back.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, angular, and polygonal, made of light gray plastic with a matte, pixelated texture. It has sharp corners and a utilitarian appearance, with no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor has an integrated, blocky base that is part of the main casing, extending downward and slightly forward, providing stability. The base is made of the same gray plastic and does not appear to be adjustable.
attribute_button_placement: A single red button is located on the lower left of the front bezel, separated from any other controls.
